zh.Rawston, Leeds, England: 英國英格蘭韋斯特 越可爾利德斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Henry de Sothill II於1300年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾素爾時，他的父親Henry de Soothill I30歲，母親Riling25歲。他於1340年與Beatrice de Néville在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1385年之後在英國越可爾，埋葬在英國越可爾巴特利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sothill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Soothill (in Dewsbury, WR Yorks).

variants.name: Suttle
meanings: nickname from Middle English sotil, sutel ‘subtle, clever, cunning’ (Old French sotil).
habitational name from Soothill in Dewsbury (Yorkshire), from Old English sōt ‘soot’ + hyll ‘hill’.

variants.name: Southwell
meanings: from Middle English south ‘south’ + wel(le) ‘well, pool, spring’ (Old English sūth + wella). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived ‘by the south well’ or ‘south of the well’, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Southwell (Nottinghamshire) or Southwell on the Isle of Portland (Dorset).
in Yorkshire and Lancashire, often a hypercorrect form of Soothill, a habitational name from Soothill in Dewsbury (Yorkshire), from Old English sōt ‘soot’ + hyll ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Henry de Sothill II was born in 1300, in Soothill, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry de Soothill I, was 30 and his mother, Riling, was 25. He married Beatrice de Néville in 1340, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died after 1385, in Yorkshire, England, and was buried in Batley, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Henry de Sothill II est né en 1300, à Soothill, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Henry de Soothill I, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Riling, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Beatrice de Néville en 1340, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé après 1385, à Everwyck, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Batley, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Henry de Sothill II 1300 in Soothill, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry de Soothill I 30 und seine Mutter Riling 25. Er hat 1340 in Yorkshire, England Beatrice de Néville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb nach 1385 in Yorkshire, England und wurde in Batley, Yorkshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Sothill IIが1300年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアソオスイルで生まれた時，父Henry de Soothill Iは30歳，母Rilingは25歳であった。1340年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでBeatrice de Névilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1385年以後にイギリスヨークシュアイアで死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアバトリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Sothill II(이)가 1300년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 소스이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry de Soothill I(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Riling(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1340년 영국 요르크실르에서 Beatrice de Néville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1385년 이후 영국 요르크실르에서 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 바틀리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Henry de Sothill II родился в 1300 г. в местности сутилл, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Henry de Soothill I было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Riling, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrice de Néville в 1340 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер после 1385 г. в местности еркшир, Англия и был похоронен в местности батли, еркшир, Англия.
